codeReview.summary: The secret-gated dry-run scaffold is wired through `mini/scripts/upload-wechat.mjs`, backed by focused regression tests, and the worker followed the Mini release-scaffold procedure. However, the checked-in live-activation path is incomplete: `mini/lib/wechat-ci-upload.js` only reaches real upload by `require('miniprogram-ci')`, but the Mini package manifest/lockfile never install that dependency, so a clean checkout still fails after all documented env vars are provided. That leaves the feature short of the promised later-activation path and makes the new handoff copy overstate what remains after secrets are injected.

issues.description: The checked-in live-upload path is not actually activatable on a clean checkout. `performLiveWechatCiUpload()` unconditionally `require`s `miniprogram-ci`, but `mini/package.json` has no dependency entry and `mini/package-lock.json` contains no `miniprogram-ci` record. Even after operators provide the documented private key, robot/version/description, and `WECHAT_MINI_CI_ENABLE_LIVE_UPLOAD=1`, the command still falls into `refused_missing_dependency` instead of reaching the promised later-activation path. This also conflicts with `mini/lib/release-handoff.js:28` and `:41`, which say the scaffold only waits on injected secrets/upload metadata.

summary: Fail. The dry-run secret gate and refusal messaging look correct, but the checked-in live path cannot be activated from a clean repo because `miniprogram-ci` is required at runtime without being declared in the Mini package manifest or lockfile.
